text.data-narration: Waste management is a critical component of environmental management, encompassing the activities and actions required to manage waste from its inception to its final disposal.
text.tspan: Waste management is a critical component of environmental management, encompassing the activities and actions required to manage waste from its inception to its final disposal.

text.data-narration: The waste hierarchy is a set of priorities that serves as the cornerstone of most waste minimization strategies. It is designed to prioritize actions that deliver the best outcome for the environment.
text.tspan: The waste hierarchy is a set of priorities that serves as the cornerstone of most waste minimization strategies. It is designed to prioritize actions that deliver the best outcome for the environment.

text.data-narration: The most effective method is to reduce waste by designing products and processes to minimize the amount of waste created. Examples include using digital documents instead of paper, buying products with less packaging, and designing longer-lasting goods.
text.tspan: The most effective method is to reduce waste by designing products and processes to minimize the amount of waste created. Examples include using digital documents instead of paper, buying products with less packaging, and designing longer-lasting goods.

text.data-narration: Reuse involves using products or materials that are still in good condition to reduce waste. This can include buying second-hand items, swapping products, or repairing damaged goods.
text.tspan: Reuse involves using products or materials that are still in good condition to reduce waste. This can include buying second-hand items, swapping products, or repairing damaged goods.

text.data-narration: Recovery involves recovering resources from waste and producing energy where possible. This can include recycling, composting, or repurposing materials.
text.tspan: Recovery involves recovering resources from waste and producing energy where possible. This can include recycling, composting, or repurposing materials.

text.data-narration: The least preferred method is to dispose of waste. This should be the last resort and should only be used when all other options have been exhausted.
text.tspan: The least preferred method is to dispose of waste. This should be the last resort and should only be used when all other options have been exhausted.

svg.text: This diagram illustrates the different stages of waste management, along with the hierarchy of priorities. By following these steps, we can reduce waste, reuse materials, recover resources, and dispose of waste responsibly.
